attractions.name: Collected Works Bookstore & Coffeehouse
attractions.description: Collected Works Bookstore & Coffeehouse is Santa Fe’s oldest locally owned independent bookstore, serving as a community hub for over 48 years. Located just blocks from the historic downtown Plaza, it offers an inviting space with thousands of titles, a full-service coffeehouse, and frequent literary events. Collected Works Bookstore

attractions.name: Gruet Winery
attractions.description: Founded in 1984, Gruet Winery is a family-owned business specializing in Méthode Champenoise sparkling wines crafted from Pinot Noir and Chardonnay grapes. With roots in Bethon, France, the winery has gained national acclaim for its premium bubbles and unique New Mexico terroir, operating elegant tasting rooms in both historic downtown Santa Fe and its main production facility in Albuquerque. Gruet Winery Heritage, Wikipedia

attractions.description: The IAIA Museum of Contemporary Native Arts (MoCNA) is the country's only museum dedicated to exhibiting, collecting, and interpreting the most progressive work of contemporary Native artists. Located in the heart of downtown Santa Fe, it stewards a premier collection of over 9,500 artworks created from 1962 to the present. IAIA MoCNA, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Museum Hill Santa Fe
attractions.description: Museum Hill is a world-class cultural destination perched on a ridge southeast of downtown Santa Fe, offering sweeping views of the Sangre de Cristo Mountains. The complex brings together four renowned museums, the Santa Fe Botanical Garden, and a scenic cafe, serving as a centralized hub for exploring Indigenous heritage, Spanish colonial influences, and global folk art traditions. Museum Hill Official, Tourism Santa Fe
attractions.address: 706 Camino Lejo (Museum Hill)
attractions.hours: Museums: Tue-Sun 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Closed Mondays November-April); Santa Fe Botanical Garden: 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM Daily (Mar-Oct), Wed-Sun 10:00 AM - 4:00 PM (Nov-Feb); Museum Hill Cafe: Tue-Sun 11:00 AM - 3:00 PM. MOIFA Visit, MIAC Visit, SFBG Visit, Museum Hill Cafe
attractions.days_open: Tuesday - Sunday (Most museums closed on Mondays)
attractions.admission: Adults: $12 (Non-Residents), $7 (NM Residents); Seniors (60+): $12 (Non-Residents), $7 (NM Residents); Students (with ID): $12 (Non-Residents), $7 (NM Residents); Youth (16 and under): Free. Admission is Free for all to the Nuevo Mexicano Heritage Arts Museum. The Museum of International Folk Art is Free during roof repairs (through May 2026). Santa Fe Botanical Garden: $16 Adults, $8 Youth (4-17), Free for Child (0-3). Wheelwright Museum: $10 General Admission, Free for children under 12 and students with ID. MOIFA Hours & Admission, MIAC Hours & Admission, Wheelwright Visit, SFBG Visit

highlights: Home to the Museum of International Folk Art, which houses the world's largest collection of folk art, including the famous Girard Wing.
The Museum of Indian Arts & Culture features over 70,000 artifacts tracing the history and creativity of Indigenous peoples of the Southwest.
The Wheelwright Museum of the American Indian, Santa Fe's oldest non-profit museum, is housed in a unique eight-sided building modeled after a Navajo Hogan.
Milner Plaza offers a public sculpture garden, a seven-circuit labyrinth, and panoramic vistas of the surrounding mountain ranges.
The Santa Fe Botanical Garden highlights the unique high-desert ecology of northern New Mexico through native plant displays and an art trail.
Weldon's Museum Hill Cafe provides a picturesque spot for lunch or brunch with views of the landscape. Tourism Santa Fe, Museum Hill Official
attractions.tips: Pick up a New Mexico CulturePass for $30 to save on admission to multiple state museums. NM Residents enjoy free admission on the first Sunday of every month, and NM Seniors visit free on Wednesdays. Plan to spend several hours or a full day to explore all institutions and enjoy lunch at the cafe. Tourism Santa Fe, Museum Hill Official

attractions.name: Nicholas Potter Bookseller
attractions.description: Nicholas Potter Bookseller was a legendary Santa Fe institution for nearly 50 years, renowned for its "old-school" approach to the book trade. Housed in a historic six-room adobe building, the shop specialized in rare and used titles across literature, art, photography, and Southwestern Americana. It was celebrated for its tactile browsing experience and its refusal to use computers or list inventory online, making it a beloved haunt for locals and famous authors alike. Princeton Alumni Weekly, Santa Fe New Mexican

attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday (Closed Sundays)
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Specializes in Southwestern Americana, fine art, photography, and literary first editions.
Housed in a charming, historic six-room adobe house near the Santa Fe Plaza.
Maintained a strictly "old-school" atmosphere with no computers or electronic inventory.
Frequent destination for notable figures including Cormac McCarthy and Ethan Coen.
Held an extensive collection of over 30,000 titles between the storefront and storage.
Member of the Antiquarian Booksellers' Association of America (ABAA) for over 40 years.
attractions.tips: Allow ample time to wander through all six rooms, including the "metaphysical closet" and the old kitchen housing cookbooks. The shop is best visited for serendipitous discoveries rather than searching for specific titles, as the proprietor encouraged the tactile experience of browsing. Note that the physical storefront closed following the owner's passing in late 2024, with portions of the collection now managed by Barbara's Collectibles in Santa Fe. Princeton Alumni Weekly, Barbara's Bookstore Collectibles

attractions.name: Santa Fe Community Convention Center
attractions.description: Located in the heart of downtown, this state-of-the-art facility is just as unique as "The City Different," featuring design elements that honor the area's ancient heritage. During its construction, excavations revealed artifacts from 13th-century Native American pueblos, which are now commemorated through a stunning tapestry displayed in the sun-drenched lobby. The building is LEED Gold Certified and offers 40,000 square feet of customizable meeting space, balancing modern technology with historic charm (Tourism Santa Fe).

attractions.name: The Cathedral Basilica of St. Francis of Assisi Official Site
attractions.description: The Cathedral Basilica of St. Francis of Assisi is a historic Roman Catholic cathedral located in downtown Santa Fe, serving as the mother church of the Archdiocese of Santa Fe. Built between 1869 and 1887 by Archbishop Jean Baptiste Lamy, the structure features a distinctive Romanesque Revival style that contrasts with the local adobe architecture. The site houses important relics and artworks, including the La Conquistadora chapel, which is the oldest part of the building dating back to 1717. Official Site, Wikipedia)

attractions.tips: The church is closed for cleaning on Mondays; photography is allowed but should be respectful of worshippers; free parking is available on the south side for visitors. Official Site

Weekly events including Saturday slide shows and language classes (mapsofnewmexico.com)

attractions.name: Georgia O'Keeffe Museum (The Georgia O'Keeffe Museum)
attractions.description: The Georgia O'Keeffe Museum is dedicated to the artistic legacy of Georgia O'Keeffe, showcasing her life and work within the context of American Modernism. It houses the largest collection of O'Keeffe's work in the world, including iconic desert landscapes, flower paintings, and personal effects (The Georgia O'Keeffe Museum, Tripadvisor).
attractions.address: 217 Johnson St, Santa Fe, NM 87501-1826 (Tripadvisor)
attractions.hours: Open daily from 10:00 AM to 5:00 PM (The Georgia O'Keeffe Museum)
attractions.admission: General Admission: $22; Museum Members: Free; Children (5 and under): Free; Youth (6-18), College Students, and Veterans receive discounted admission (typically ~$11-18 depending on residency and status); NM Residents: Free on First Fridays (The Georgia O'Keeffe Museum, Tripadvisor)
highlights: Selected works from every era of O’Keeffe’s career, including her early watercolors and bold abstractions (The Georgia O'Keeffe Museum)
A collection of the artist's personal belongings and artifacts (Tripadvisor)
Rotating thematic galleries that explore her evolving artistic journey (TheCollector)
Free self-guided audio tours available through the Bloomberg Connects app (The Georgia O'Keeffe Museum)
The Michael S. Engl Family Foundation Library and Archive (visits by appointment) (The Georgia O'Keeffe Museum)
attractions.tips: Advance timed-entry tickets are strongly recommended as the museum often sells out. Bring your own earbuds for the free audio tour. Large bags and backpacks are not permitted, but lockers are available free of charge (The Georgia O'Keeffe Museum, Tripadvisor).
attractions.nearby: Cathedral Basilica of St. Francis of Assisi, New Mexico State Capitol, New Mexico Museum of Art, Palace of the Governors, and the Historic Santa Fe Plaza (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: La Fonda on the Plaza (Official Site)
attractions.description: La Fonda on the Plaza is a historic luxury hotel situated in the heart of Santa Fe, serving as a vibrant landmark that has set the standard for hospitality since 1922. As the only hotel located directly on the historic Santa Fe Plaza, it features authentic Pueblo Revival architecture and a world-class collection of Southwestern art. (Official Site, Wikipedia)
attractions.address: 100 E. San Francisco St., Santa Fe, NM 87501 (Official Site)
attractions.hours: Hotel: 24/7; La Plazuela (Main Restaurant): Breakfast Daily 7:00 am–10:30 am, Lunch Mon-Fri 11:00 am–2:00 pm (Sat-Sun 3:00 pm), Dinner Daily 5:00 pm–9:00 pm (Official Site, AAA)
attractions.admission: Free (for entrance to the hotel and lobby); Docent-led Art & History tours are Complimentary but require reservation (Official Site)
highlights: Member of Historic Hotels of America and a former Harvey House (Wikipedia)
Home to the acclaimed La Plazuela restaurant, known for its Southwestern cuisine and unique skylit courtyard (Official Site)
Features a significant collection of original Southwestern artwork throughout its lobby and guestrooms (Official Site)
Seasonal Bell Tower Bar offers iconic sunset views overlooking the Santa Fe Plaza (Tripadvisor)
Complimentary docent-led art and history tours available 4 days a week (Official Site)
The only hotel situated directly on the historic Santa Fe Plaza (Official Site)
attractions.tips: Visit the Bell Tower Rooftop Bar early to secure a seat for the sunset, as it is a popular spot and fills up quickly. Take advantage of the complimentary art and history tours (Wednesday–Saturday at 10:30 am) to fully appreciate the hotel's rich heritage and extensive art collection. (Official Site, My Home and Travels)
attractions.nearby: Santa Fe Plaza, Palace of the Governors, Loretto Chapel, Cathedral Basilica of St. Francis of Assisi (Official Site, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: New Mexico History Museum New Mexico History Museum

attractions.description: The New Mexico History Museum is a premier cultural complex in Santa Fe that chronicles the state's rich and diverse narrative from ancient Native American cultures to the present day. It encompasses the historic Palace of the Governors, the oldest public building in continuous use in the United States, alongside modern exhibition spaces and research facilities. New Mexico History Museum, New Mexico Culture
attractions.address: 113 Lincoln Avenue, Santa Fe, NM 87501 New Mexico History Museum
attractions.city: Santa Fe New Mexico History Museum
attractions.region: Historic Santa Fe Plaza / Downtown New Mexico History Museum
attractions.hours: Open Daily: 10:00 am – 5:00 pm (May–October); Tuesday–Sunday: 10:00 am – 5:00 pm (November–April). Open until 7:00 pm on the first Friday of each month. New Mexico History Museum
attractions.days_open: Daily (May through October); Tuesday through Sunday (November through April) New Mexico History Museum
attractions.admission: Non-residents: $12; New Mexico Residents: $7; Children (16 and under): Free; New Mexico Residents on first Sunday of the month: Free; New Mexico Seniors (60+) on Wednesdays: Free; Military (Blue Star Program, Armed Forces Day through Labor Day): Free; All visitors on First Friday evenings (5-7 p.m.): Free; CulturePass (access to 15 sites): $30 New Mexico History Museum, New Mexico Culture
highlights: Palace of the Governors: The oldest public building in continuous use in the U.S., built in 1610. New Mexico History Museum
Segesser Hide Paintings: Rare 18th-century paintings on bison and elk hides depicting early colonial conflicts. New Mexico History Museum
Native American Artisans Portal Program: A tradition where Native artisans display and sell handcrafted work under the Palace portal. New Mexico History Museum
Fray Angélico Chávez History Library: A major research facility for Southwest and Meso-American history. New Mexico History Museum
Palace Print Shop & Bindery: A working exhibit dedicated to New Mexico's historic printing traditions. New Mexico History Museum
Photo Archives: A collection of over 1 million historic photographs documenting the region's history. New Mexico History Museum
attractions.tips: Allow at least 2-3 hours to explore the three-building campus. Metered parking is available throughout the downtown area, and the museum is within walking distance of many hotels. Consider purchasing the New Mexico CulturePass if you plan to visit multiple state museums or historic sites. New Mexico History Museum, New Mexico Culture
attractions.nearby: Santa Fe Plaza, New Mexico Museum of Art, Loretto Chapel, Cathedral Basilica of Saint Francis of Assisi, Georgia O'Keeffe Museum, New Mexico State Capitol Santa Fe Tourism, Wikipedia

attractions.category: Art Museum New Mexico Museum of Art
attractions.description: Established in 1917, the New Mexico Museum of Art is the oldest art museum in the state and is housed in an iconic Pueblo Revival-style building. Its collection of over 20,000 works focuses on American art, particularly by artists who lived and worked in the Southwest, including the Taos Society of Artists and Georgia O'Keeffe. The museum recently expanded its footprint with the opening of the Vladem Contemporary in the Santa Fe Railyard District. New Mexico Museum of Art, Wikipedia

attractions.city: Santa Fe New Mexico Museum of Art

attractions.days_open: May through October: Open daily; November through April: Tuesday through Sunday (Closed Mondays) New Mexico Museum of Art
attractions.admission: General admission: $12; New Mexico residents: $7; Children 16 and under: Free; NM Seniors (60+) on Wednesdays: Free; NM residents on first Sundays: Free; NM residents on Fridays 5–7 PM (May-Oct): Free; Museum of NM Foundation members and NM foster families: Free New Mexico Museum of Art
highlights: Iconic Pueblo Revival architecture of the 1917 Plaza Building New Mexico Museum of Art
Extensive collections of the Taos Society of Artists and Los Cinco Pintores New Mexico Museum of Art
St. Francis Auditorium, featuring historic murals and a 1917 McNary pipe organ New Mexico Museum of Art

New Mexico Museum of Art

attractions.name: The Shed Restaurant The Shed
attractions.description: The Shed is a landmark Santa Fe dining institution that has been family-owned and operated since 1953. Housed in the historic Prince Patio adobe hacienda dating back to 1692, the restaurant is world-renowned for its authentic Northern New Mexican cuisine, particularly its red chile enchiladas and blue corn specialties. The Shed, All Things Andy Gavin
attractions.address: 113½ East Palace Avenue, Santa Fe, NM 87501 The Shed
attractions.region: Santa Fe Plaza / Downtown Year of the Monkey
attractions.hours: Monday - Saturday: Lunch 11:00 AM - 2:30 PM, Dinner 5:00 PM - 9:00 PM (Bar opens at 4:00 PM); Sunday: Closed The Shed

Historic 1692 adobe hacienda setting The Shed
Awarded the James Beard Foundation\
America\'s Classics
attractions.tips: It is highly recommended to book dinner reservations well in advance as the restaurant is extremely popular; lunch is walk-in only and often has long wait times. If waiting, the bar opens at 4:00 PM and is a great spot for a signature margarita. Consider ordering your dish "Christmas" style to sample both the famous red and green chiles. The Shed, Bon Traveler

attractions.name: Canyon Road Art District (Visit Canyon Road)
attractions.description: Canyon Road is a world-renowned art district in Santa Fe, New Mexico, featuring over a hundred galleries, boutiques, and restaurants within a magical half-mile stretch. Nestled into the foothills of the Sangre de Cristo Mountains, this historic district offers a diverse collection of fine art ranging from traditional Western and Native American works to cutting-edge contemporary and abstract pieces (Visit Canyon Road).
attractions.address: Canyon Road (stretching between Paseo de Peralta and East Palace Avenue), Santa Fe, NM 87501 (Visit Canyon Road)
attractions.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Tuesday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Wednesday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Thursday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Friday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Sunday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Most galleries follow these hours; restaurants and specific galleries may differ) (Visit Canyon Road)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Gallery individual schedules vary, with most open 7 days a week) (Visit Canyon Road)
attractions.admission: Free (Individual galleries may vary, but strolling the district and visiting most galleries is free) (Santa Fe Art Tours)
attractions.phone: 505-982-1648 (Santa Fe Gallery Association) (Santa Fe Gallery Association)
highlights: Highest density of art galleries in the United States, with over 100 establishments (TOURISM Santa Fe)
Historic adobe architecture and charming tree-lined pedestrian-friendly streets (Visit Canyon Road)
Lyman Whitaker’s iconic wind sculptures at Wiford Gallery (TravelistaLiz)
Diverse art styles including Native American, Western, Latino, and Contemporary (Wikipedia)
The annual Christmas Eve Farolito Walk, a beloved local holiday tradition (See Great Art)
Exquisite dining options like Geronimo and The Teahouse (Visit Canyon Road)
attractions.tips: Wear comfortable walking shoes as the historic sidewalks can be uneven; stay hydrated due to the high altitude (7,200 ft); and consider visiting the first Friday of the month for gallery openings and events (Santa Fe Art Tours).
attractions.nearby: The Santa Fe Plaza, Museum Hill (home to the Museum of International Folk Art), Santa Fe River Park, and the Sangre de Cristo Mountains (Visit Canyon Road)

attractions.name: Canyon Road Farolito Walk (visitcanyonroad.com)
attractions.description: The Canyon Road Farolito Walk is a cherished Santa Fe holiday tradition where thousands of flickering paper bag lanterns (farolitos) line the historic adobe streets. The event transforms the world-class gallery district into a glowing winter wonderland, featuring bonfires, caroling, and festive refreshments. (visitcanyonroad.com, SantaFe.com)
attractions.address: Canyon Road, Acequia Madre, and nearby streets
attractions.region: Canyon Road Historic District / East Side (TOURISM Santa Fe)
attractions.hours: Sundown to approximately 9:00 PM or 10:00 PM (SantaFe.com, Travel and Tour World)
attractions.admission: Free (SantaFe.com, Santa Fe NM True)
attractions.phone: 800-777-2489 (Tourism Santa Fe) or 505-982-1648 (Santa Fe Gallery Association) (Santa Fe Gallery Association)
highlights: Thousands of farolitos (paper bag lanterns) lighting up adobe walls and rooftops (visitcanyonroad.com)
Luminarias (small piñon wood bonfires) providing warmth and a signature aroma (SantaFe.com)
Live caroling and holiday music performed by street musicians (Travel and Tour World)
Complimentary hot chocolate, cider, and biscochitos offered by local galleries (SantaFe.com)
Historic adobe architecture backdrop including over 100 world-class art galleries (TOURISM Santa Fe)
attractions.tips: Dress in warm layers and wear comfortable walking shoes as the terrain can be uneven or icy. Arrive early for parking or consider walking from downtown, as streets are closed to vehicle traffic. Leave strollers and large bags at home due to dense crowds. (SantaFe.com)
attractions.nearby: Historic District of Santa Fe, Acequia Madre neighborhood, Santa Fe Plaza, Cristo Rey Church, Patrick Smith Park (visitcanyonroad.com, TOURISM Santa Fe)

attractions.name: Geronimo
attractions.description: Geronimo is an elegant fine-dining establishment located in a historic 1756 adobe home known as the "Borrego House" on Santa Fe's famous Canyon Road. The restaurant features a "Global Eclectic" menu created by Executive Chef Sllin Cruz, which expertly blends Southwestern, Asian, and Mediterranean flavors in a cozy, upscale atmosphere. (Geronimo, Forbes Travel Guide)
attractions.address: 724 Canyon Road
attractions.hours: Daily: 4:45 PM - Close (typically around 11:00 PM) (Tripadvisor, Geronimo)
attractions.admission: Vegetarian Tasting: $80 ($120 with wine pairing); Main Courses: $44 - $64
highlights: Recipient of the AAA Four Diamond and Forbes Travel Guide Four Star Awards. (Geronimo)
Housed in the historic Borrego House, built in 1756 by Geronimo Lopez. (New Mexico Magazine)
Signature "Global Eclectic" menu featuring dishes like Tellicherry Rubbed Elk Tenderloin and Green Miso Sea Bass. (OpenTable)
Cozy interior design with thick adobe walls, kiva fireplaces, and wood beams. (Forbes Travel Guide)
Ranked among the "Top 100 Restaurants in the Country" by OpenTable and TripAdvisor. (Geronimo)
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended; children under 10 are not permitted. The dress code is business casual, and valet parking is available just past the restaurant. (OpenTable, Forbes Travel Guide)
attractions.nearby: Canyon Road Art Galleries, Santa Fe Plaza, The Compound Restaurant, El Farol (Gil's Thrilling Blog, New Mexico Magazine)

attractions.name: The Compound Restaurant
attractions.description: The Compound Restaurant is an elegant, heritage-rich fine dining establishment renowned for its distinctive Alexander Girard-designed interiors and sophisticated Southwestern-meets-Mediterranean cuisine. Since opening in 1966, it has become a cornerstone of the Santa Fe culinary landscape, offering seasonal menus led by James Beard award-winning Chef Mark Kiffin. (Official Website, Gwen Books)
attractions.address: 653 Canyon Rd, Santa Fe, NM 87501-2762
attractions.hours: Lunch: Monday - Saturday, 12:00 PM - 2:00 PM; Dinner: Monday - Sunday, 5:00 PM - 7:30 PM (or 8:30 PM per some listings); Bar service available (Official Website, Tripadvisor).
attractions.admission: Fine dining; entrees typically $30-$55+; typical price per person $50-$100+ (Tripadvisor, Well Traveled). Free to visit for bar/patio service.
highlights: James Beard award-winning culinary team and seasonal American fine dining menu (Official Website).
Mid-century modern interiors designed by famed architect Alexander Girard, featuring his signature folk art and fabrics (Official Website).
Acclaimed wine program, recipient of the Wine Spectator Best of Award of Excellence (Official Website).
AAA Four Diamond designation and consistent recognition as one of Santa Fe's top restaurants (Official Website, Tripadvisor).
Signature dishes including Chicken Schnitzel, Lobster & Mango Salad, and Sweetbreads with Foie Gras (Gwen Books).
Charming outdoor patios and a historic adobe building located on the iconic Canyon Road (Official Website).
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended, especially during peak season, though walk-ins are welcomed. For a more casual experience, consider the bar or patio dining in warmer weather (Official Website, Tripadvisor). Don't miss the unique 'conversation pit' bar designed by Alexander Girard (Gwen Books).
attractions.nearby: Canyon Road Art Galleries, Santa Fe River Park, Geronimo, The Teahouse, Ahmyo Wine Garden (Tripadvisor).

attractions.name: Waxlander Art Gallery & Sculpture Garden
attractions.description: Waxlander Art Gallery & Sculpture Garden was a landmark destination on Santa Fe's iconic Canyon Road for over 30 years, founded by artist Phyllis Kapp in 1985. The gallery was renowned for its vibrant and joyful collection of contemporary art, representing over 30 artists across 15 exhibition rooms and a scenic sculpture garden. It was a central fixture in the Santa Fe art scene, known for its participation in local traditions like the Edible Art Tour and the Farolito Walk. (Waxlander Gallery, Southwest Art Magazine)

attractions.hours: Open Daily 9:30 AM – 5:30 PM (Historical) (Waxlander Gallery)

Features a spacious 15-room gallery layout in a historic building (Waxlander Gallery)
Includes an outdoor sculpture garden showcasing three-dimensional works (Waxlander Gallery)
Showcases the exuberant watercolor landscapes of founder Phyllis Kapp (Southwest Art Magazine)
Recognized as a long-standing landmark in the Canyon Road arts district (Sharing Santa Fe)
attractions.tips: Parking on Canyon Road is limited; visitors are advised to use nearby metered lots or walk from the Historic District. Be sure to explore all 15 rooms of the gallery, as each room offers a distinct artistic atmosphere. (Waxlander Gallery, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: LewAllen Galleries
attractions.description: LewAllen Galleries is one of the Southwest's oldest and largest destinations for contemporary and modern art, located in an award-winning 14,000-square-foot space in Santa Fe's Railyard Arts District. The gallery represents internationally acclaimed artists across various media, including painting, sculpture, and world-class studio glass. Its museum-like setting hosts a robust schedule of rotating exhibitions that bridge the region's historical modernism with cutting-edge contemporary works. (New Mexico Tourism, YouTube)

attractions.description: Second Street Brewery is a celebrated local fixture in Santa Fe, operating since 1996 and known for its award-winning craft beers and vibrant atmosphere. The brewery features multiple locations, including a bustling taproom in the Railyard Arts District and a larger production facility and taproom on Rufina Street. It serves a diverse menu of high-quality pub fare and New Mexican specialties, often accompanied by live music and community events.

attractions.description: Edwina and Charles Milner Plaza is an expansive outdoor gathering space on Museum Hill that serves as a central hub for four world-class museums. It features panoramic views of the Sangre de Cristo and Jemez Mountains, beautifully manicured native gardens, and various outdoor sculptures (Milestone Imports, jmarshallplan.com).

Home to a unique porphyry stone labyrinth (Labyrinth Resource Group)
Primary venue for the annual International Folk Art Market in July (International Folk Art Market)
Panoramic views of the Sangre de Cristo and Jemez Mountain ranges (New Mexico Culture)

Site of community events like the Winter Glow Holiday Stroll (Santa Fe NM True)

Visit during the third Wednesday of the month for curator identification sessions at the adjacent museums (Indian Arts & Culture).

attractions.name: Museum of Indian Arts and Culture
attractions.description: The Museum of Indian Arts and Culture serves as a premier repository of Native art and material culture, interpreting the history and contemporary life of the Pueblo, Navajo, Apache, and other Indigenous cultures of the Southwest. Its extensive collection includes over 70,000 artifacts, ranging from ancestral pottery to contemporary fine art and textiles. Official Website, Tripadvisor
attractions.address: 710 Camino Lejo, Santa Fe, NM 87505-7511
attractions.hours: Monday–Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (May–October); Tuesday–Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (November–April). Closed on January 1, Thanksgiving, and December 25. Official Website

highlights: Here, Now and Always: A groundbreaking permanent exhibition featuring over 600 objects and Indigenous narratives.

Outdoor Sculpture Garden: Featuring rotating exhibits of monumental works by Native American sculptors.
Laboratory of Anthropology: A research center and library focused on the archaeology and ethnology of the Southwest.

Milner Plaza Labyrinth: A unique outdoor feature with distinctive acoustics and panoramic mountain views.

Free docent-led tours are often available daily, but it is recommended to call ahead to confirm schedules. Official Website, Tripadvisor

attractions.description: The Nuevo Mexicano Heritage Arts Museum (formerly the Museum of Spanish Colonial Art) is the only museum in the world devoted solely to the interpretation and exhibition of New Mexico's Hispano heritage arts. Housed in a historic 1930 Pueblo-Spanish Revival residence designed by renowned architect John Gaw Meem, it features an extensive collection of over 4,000 objects spanning five centuries and four continents, including traditional religious art, furniture, and contemporary works. Nestled in the foothills of the Sangre de Cristo Mountains, the garden unfolds over nearly 20 acres and features immersive educational events, scientific exploration, and inviting pathways of diverse plant species. Founded in 1937, the museum is housed in a unique octagonal building designed after a traditional Navajo hooghan (hogan) and features world-renowned collections of jewelry, textiles, and basketry. Wheelwright Museum, Idealist

It serves as a cultural time capsule, preserving vast collections of art, archives, and historical artifacts that chronicle over 150 years of family and New Mexican history. attractions.name: Santa Fe Historic District (Downtown and Eastside Historic District)
attractions.description: The Santa Fe Historic District (also known as the Downtown and Eastside Historic District) is the cultural and architectural heart of Santa Fe, encompassing the central plaza and surrounding neighborhoods. It is renowned for its iconic "Santa Fe Style" architecture, which blends Spanish-Pueblo and Territorial Revival influences into a cohesive, earth-toned streetscape. The district preserves over four centuries of history, from its founding in 1610 to its role as the oldest state capital in the United States.
attractions.address: Roughly bounded by Camino Cabra, Camino de las Animas, W. Manhattan Ave., S. St. Francis Dr., and Griffin St. (Paseo de Peralta forms a central boundary loop)
attractions.hours: Accessible 24 hours daily (Individual attractions and shops typically open from 10:00 AM to 5:00 PM)
attractions.admission: Free (Individual museums and sites within the district have separate admission fees)
attractions.phone: (505) 955-6605 (City of Santa Fe Historic Preservation Division)
highlights: Santa Fe Plaza: The historic market square and central gathering place of the city since 1610.
Palace of the Governors: The oldest continuously occupied public building in the United States, now a history museum.
Loretto Chapel: Famous for its "Miraculous Staircase," a wooden spiral stair with no visible means of support.
Canyon Road: A half-mile stretch of historic adobe homes now housing over 100 art galleries and studios.
Cathedral Basilica of St. Francis of Assisi: A landmark Romanesque Revival cathedral built in the late 19th century.
Georgia O'Keeffe Museum: A major cultural institution dedicated to the life and artistic legacy of one of America's most iconic painters.
attractions.tips: The district is extremely walkable and best explored on foot to appreciate the narrow side streets and hidden courtyards. Be sure to stay hydrated and pace yourself, as the city sits at an altitude of over 7,000 feet. For authentic local crafts, visit the artisans selling jewelry and pottery under the portal of the Palace of the Governors.

attractions.description: The Railyard Park is a 13-acre award-winning urban green space in the heart of Santa Fe, featuring 28 individual gardens, native plantings, and a functional acequia. It serves as a community gathering place with walking paths, public art, and a children's play area, transformed from an abandoned railyard into a sustainable public asset. Official Website, Tourism Santa Fe

Now serving as an art and history museum, it houses a magnificent 1783 altar screen by Jose de Alzibar and a significant collection of Spanish Colonial and New Mexican religious art. Tourism Santa Fe

attractions.name: The Santa Fe Opera
attractions.description: The Santa Fe Opera is a world-renowned American opera company known for its unique open-air Crosby Theatre and stunning mountain views. Founded in 1956, it presents a summer festival season featuring standard repertoire alongside world and American premieres.

attractions.name: Bandelier National Monument National Park Service
attractions.description: Bandelier National Monument protects over 33,000 acres of ruggedly beautiful canyon and mesa country, preserving the homes and territory of the Ancestral Puebloans. The site features petroglyphs, dwellings carved into soft rock cliffs (cavates), and standing masonry walls dating between 1150 and 1600 AD. Visitors can explore the landscape through over 70 miles of hiking trails, many of which offer the opportunity to climb wooden ladders into ancient cliff dwellings. National Park Service, Wikipedia

attractions.hours: Visitor Center: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM daily; Park: Dawn to Dusk daily National Park Service

Tsankawi Unit: A separate section of the park with deep foot-worn trails and unexcavated ruins. National Park Service
attractions.tips: Arrive before 9 AM or after 3 PM to avoid the mandatory shuttle from White Rock (typically mid-June to mid-October). The park is cashless, so bring a credit or debit card for fees. Pets are not allowed on any park trails. National Park Service, A Passion and A Passport

attractions.name: Bradbury Science Museum
attractions.description: As Los Alamos National Laboratory’s official museum, the Bradbury Science Museum helps visitors learn about the Lab’s beginnings during the Manhattan Project and how the Lab’s continuing work enables global security. The facility features approximately 60 interactive exhibits across three distinct galleries focused on History, Defense, and Research. Visitors can explore the scientific breakthroughs of the Atomic Age and the Laboratory's current contributions to modern science and technology.

attractions.tips: Personal photography is allowed, but commercial media must contact the lab for permission. Visitors should plan for alternative transportation as rideshare services like Uber and Lyft generally do not operate in Los Alamos. Allow at least 2–3 hours to fully explore the interactive exhibits and view the films. Official Website, Tripadvisor

attractions.description: Collected Works Bookstore & Coffeehouse is Santa Fe's oldest locally owned independent bookstore, having served the community for over 48 years. Located just blocks from the historic downtown Plaza, the store features an extensive inventory with a strong emphasis on Southwest history, Native American culture, and local authors. The space also includes a popular in-store coffeehouse serving locally-roasted Iconik coffee, making it a beloved community hub for literary events and gatherings (Official Site, Santa Fe Selection).

attractions.name: Coyote Cafe & Cantina (Coyote Cafe)

The establishment features a sophisticated fine-dining room and a more casual, year-round rooftop Cantina offering vibrant views of the city's historic adobe walls (Coyote Cafe, Tripadvisor). It is celebrated for its creative use of regional, native ingredients in contemporary dishes that have earned it a worldwide following (Coyote Cafe).

It is situated on a hilltop in Fort Marcy Park, offering a scenic overlook and a series of historical plaques that detail Santa Fe's 400-year history (City of Santa Fe, Historic Santa Fe Foundation).

attractions.description: The Eight Northern Indian Pueblos Arts and Crafts Show is the largest Native-owned and operated outdoor arts exhibition and market in the western United States. It features hundreds of Native artists showcasing traditional pottery, jewelry, weaving, and painting, while celebrating the shared cultural heritages of the nineteen New Mexico pueblos and other tribes. ICT News, Tourism Santa Fe

attractions.region: Rio Arriba County / Northern New Mexico

Large-scale market with over 300 Native American artists and artisans.
Traditional Pueblo foods such as Indian tacos, frybread, and oven bread.
Juried art competition with awards for excellence in various media.
Hosted at the historic Ohkay Owingeh Pueblo (formerly San Juan Pueblo).

attractions.name: El Rancho de las Golondrinas (Official Site)
attractions.description: El Rancho de las Golondrinas is a 500-acre living history museum dedicated to the heritage and culture of 18th and 19th-century New Mexico. Originally a historic paraje (rest stop) on the Camino Real, it features over 30 historic buildings and costumed villagers who demonstrate traditional lifeways like blacksmithing, weaving, and milling. (Official Site, National Park Service)

attractions.hours: Regular Season (June–November): 9:00 AM – 3:00 PM (Wednesday–Sunday); May Weekends: 9:00 AM – 3:00 PM; Festivals: Often 10:00 AM – 4:00 PM or 5:00 PM (Specific dates apply) (Official Site)
attractions.days_open: Regular Season (June 3 – November 8): Wednesday–Sunday; May: Select Weekends (Saturday and Sunday); April: Private tours/school visits only (by reservation) (Official Site)
attractions.admission: Adults: $10; Seniors (62+), Teens (13–17), Veterans, & Students (w/ ID, under 25): $8; Children 12 and under: Free; Museum & NARM Members, Blue Star Families: Free; NM Residents: Free on Fridays (June–October) (Official Site)
highlights: 34 historic buildings including original 18th-century structures (Tourism Santa Fe)
Costumed interpreters demonstrating traditional crafts and ranch activities (Wikipedia)

Interactive experiences like bread baking in traditional adobe hornos (Tourism Santa Fe)
attractions.tips: Wear walking shoes and sun protection; the terrain is hilly and rugged at 6,000 ft elevation. Allow at least 2 hours for a full visit and arrive early for the 9:30 AM daily tour. (Official Site)

attractions.name: El Santuario de Chimayo (NPS)
attractions.description: El Santuario de Chimayo is a Roman Catholic church and National Historic Landmark in Chimayo, New Mexico, renowned as one of the most important pilgrimage sites in the United States. Built between 1813 and 1816, the small adobe structure is famous for "holy dirt" from a small pit called el pocito, which is believed by many to have miraculous healing powers (NPS, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 15 Santuario Drive, Chimayo, NM 87522 (Official Site)
attractions.region: Santa Fe area, El Potrero village, Sangre de Cristo Mountains (NPS, Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Daily: 9:00 am – 5:00 pm (Official Site)
attractions.admission: Free (Official Site, Tripadvisor)
highlights: El Pocito (the "little well") containing "holy dirt" believed to have healing powers (Wikipedia).
Lord of Esquipulas Chapel, featuring 19th-century Hispanic religious folk art and a 6-foot crucifix (NPS, Wikipedia).
Holy Child of Atocha Chapel, built in 1857 and a destination for Easter pilgrimages (Official Site).
Twin front towers with belfries and elegant doors carved by Pedro Dominguez (NPS, Wikipedia).
Prayer room filled with ex-votos, discarded crutches, and testimonials of healings (Wikipedia).
attractions.tips: Photography and filming are strictly prohibited inside the chapels (Official Site). Visitors are asked to dress and behave respectfully in this active place of worship (Official Site). Free guided tours are often available on Fridays (Official Site).

attractions.name: Garcia Street Books (Garcia Street Books)
attractions.description: Garcia Street Books is a locally owned independent bookstore that has been a landmark in Santa Fe's historic East Side for over 30 years. It is celebrated for its carefully curated collection of art, regional history, and literary fiction, often displayed with a unique "covers-facing-forward" approach to inspire discovery. (Garcia Street Books, IndieBound)

attractions.hours: Monday–Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Garcia Street Books)

Specializes in well-curated selections of art, Southwest history, and unique lifestyle books (Bookshop.org).
Features a distinctive "covers-facing-forward" display style to encourage tactile browsing and discovery (Bookshop.org).
Regularly hosts local author signings and spiritual or community-focused workshops (Garcia Street Books).
Offers a localized selection of greeting cards, journals, and handmade pottery (The Indie Bob Spot).
attractions.tips: Visit the popular "Downtown Subscription" cafe right next door to enjoy your new book in their beautiful garden seating area. Check the outdoor bins for discounted books and unique local cards. (Tripadvisor)
attractions.nearby: Downtown Subscription (Cafe), Canyon Road Art District, Nedra Matteucci Galleries, San Miguel Chapel, New Mexico State Capitol (Tripadvisor)

The destination features a 70,000 square-foot casino, a 27-hole award-winning golf course, and a full-service spa inspired by Native American traditions. Guests can enjoy upscale accommodations, fine dining at Red Sage, and an extensive collection of one-of-a-kind Native American art. Hilton Buffalo Thunder, Hilton Official Site

attractions.name: Hyde Memorial State Park
attractions.description: Hyde Memorial State Park is New Mexico's first state park, nestled in the Sangre de Cristo Mountains pine forest along Little Tesuque Creek. At elevations ranging from 8,300 to 9,400 feet, the 350-acre park offers scenic hiking trails, year-round camping in yurts and sites, and popular winter activities like sledding and snowshoeing. Official Site, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 740 Hyde Park Road
attractions.hours: 7:30 AM to 4:00 PM daily (Visitor Center/Office); Park gates typically open 6:00 AM to 9:00 PM for day use. Official Site, Waze
attractions.admission: New Mexico Resident Day-Use: $5 (Exempt Oct 1st – Apr 30th); Non-Resident Day-Use: $10. Camping (per vehicle/night): Resident Primitive $10, Resident Developed $15, Non-Resident Primitive $15, Non-Resident Developed $20. Utility fees: Water $5, Electric $10. Yurt Rental: $150 (Peak May-Sep), $80 (Off-peak). Official Fees, Reserve America
highlights: New Mexico's first state park, established in 1938 and listed on the National Register of Historic Places. Wikipedia
Extensive trail system including the East and West Circle Trails and the Waterfall Trail. Official Site
Unique year-round overnight stays in three cozy, heater-equipped yurts with stargazing skylights. Official Site
Dedicated winter recreation areas for sledding, tubing, and cross-country skiing. Tourism Santa Fe
Excellent group facilities including a historic lodge and multiple picnic shelters. Official Site
Abundant wildlife viewing opportunities for deer, fox, and over 150 species of birds. Outdoorsy RV Guide
attractions.tips: There is no WiFi or cellular service within the park. Bring plenty of water as the high altitude (up to 9,400 ft) can cause dehydration, and be prepared for bears by using provided bear-proof boxes. Winter visitors should bring their own sleds/tubes as none are available for rent on-site. Official Site, Tourism Santa Fe, Outdoorsy RV Guide
attractions.nearby: Ten Thousand Waves (spa), Ski Santa Fe, Santa Fe National Forest, Canyon Road art district, and the historic Santa Fe Plaza. Tripadvisor, Wikipedia

attractions.name: Kasha-Katuwe Tent Rocks National Monument
attractions.description: Kasha-Katuwe Tent Rocks National Monument is a stunning geological area on the Pajarito Plateau, famous for its cone-shaped "tent rock" formations. These unique hoodoos were sculpted by wind and water from volcanic ash and tuff deposits layered millions of years ago, creating a dramatic landscape of slot canyons and high-desert vistas. Bureau of Land Management
attractions.address: 1101 NM 22 (Cochiti Visitor Center)
attractions.hours: 8:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Visitor check-in: 8:00 AM – 1:00 PM; last pilot vehicle at 1:00 PM). Closed Tuesday and Wednesday. Pueblo de Cochiti
attractions.days_open: Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday, Monday Bureau of Land Management
attractions.admission: Total entry requires two separate fees: a BLM Reservation ($5.00 for adults 17+, Free for ages 16 and under) and a Cochiti Pueblo Tribal Access Pass ($20.00 for adults 17+, $10.00 for juniors 2-16). Interagency Pass holders are exempt from the BLM fee but must still pay the Tribal Access fee. Recreation.gov, Purplepass
attractions.phone: 505-761-8700 (BLM Rio Puerco Field Office), 505-584-0627 (Cochiti Visitor Center) Bureau of Land Management
highlights: Slot Canyon Trail featuring narrow passageways and a steep climb to a mesa top overlook.
Cave Loop Trail, an easy 1.2-mile hike with views of the unique rock formations and a large cliff cave.
Veterans' Memorial Overlook, providing panoramic views of the Sangre de Cristo, Jemez, and Sandia mountains.
Distinctive hoodoos (tent rocks) ranging from a few feet to 90 feet in height.
Geological formations created by explosive volcanic eruptions 6 to 7 million years ago.
Rich cultural history as ancestral lands of the Pueblo de Cochiti.
attractions.tips: Reservations are mandatory and must be secured via Recreation.gov (BLM) and Purplepass (Tribal Access) before arrival. All visitors must check in at the Cochiti Visitor Center and wait for a pilot vehicle to escort them to the monument. Pets and drones are strictly prohibited. Wear sturdy hiking boots and bring at least 2 liters of water per person, as the trails are exposed and can be very hot. Recreation.gov
attractions.nearby: Cochiti Lake, Bandelier National Monument, Santa Fe, Albuquerque, Santo Domingo Pueblo. Recreation.gov

attractions.name: Lensic Performing Arts Center
attractions.description: The Lensic Performing Arts Center is a restored historic theater in downtown Santa Fe that originally opened in 1931 as a vaudeville stage and movie palace. Today, it serves as the city's premier year-round venue for world-class theater, music, dance, films, and lectures, hosting more than 200 events annually (Lensic.org). The venue is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to enriching the community through diverse and accessible programming (Lensic.org).
attractions.address: 211 W. San Francisco Street, Santa Fe, NM 87501
attractions.hours: Box Office: Monday–Friday, 10 am–5 pm; Saturday, noon–4 pm; Sunday, closed. On event days, the Box Office remains open until showtime (or opens at noon on Sundays) (Lensic.org).
attractions.admission: Varies by performance. Tickets for most shows typically range from $25 to $100+ depending on the event and seating tier. Some community and education events are offered for free or at reduced rates (Lensic.org).
highlights: Historic 1931 pseudo-Moorish and Spanish Renaissance architectural design by the Boller Brothers (Wikipedia).
State-of-the-art lighting and sound systems installed during a comprehensive $9 million restoration between 1999 and 2001 (Lensic.org).
Recognized as an official project of "Save America’s Treasures" by the National Trust for Historic Preservation (Lensic.org).
Primary home for prestigious groups such as the Santa Fe Chamber Music Festival, the Santa Fe Symphony, and Aspen Santa Fe Ballet (Wikipedia).
Intimate 821-seat venue that maintains its original charm while providing modern amenities for performers and audiences (Lensic.org).
attractions.tips: Purchase tickets only through the official website or Box Office to avoid scams. Patrons can have parking tickets validated for a $7 rate at the Sandoval or Convention Center garages. The Lensic is a fragrance-free environment and primarily uses digital tickets for entry. Arrive at least 30 minutes early, as late arrivals are seated at the discretion of the house manager (Lensic.org).
attractions.nearby: Santa Fe Plaza, Georgia O'Keeffe Museum, New Mexico Museum of Art, Loretto Chapel, Palace of the Governors (Lensic.org).

attractions.name: Loretto Chapel Official Site
attractions.description: The Loretto Chapel is a former Roman Catholic church in Santa Fe, New Mexico, renowned for its "miraculous" helix-shaped spiral staircase. Completed in 1878, the Gothic Revival structure features beautiful stained glass imported from France and is now operated as a private museum and wedding venue. The staircase remains a mystery due to its two 360-degree turns and lack of visible central support, attracting visitors from around the world Wikipedia, Official Site.

attractions.region: Downtown / Santa Fe Historic District Wikipedia

attractions.days_open: Daily (364 days a year; closed on Christmas Day) Official Site
attractions.admission: Adult Admission: $5; Seniors (65+): $4; Youth (7-17): $3; Children (under 7): Free Official Site
highlights: The Miraculous Staircase with two 360-degree turns and no central support Official Site
Stunning Gothic Revival architecture modeled after the Sainte-Chapelle in Paris Wikipedia
Beautiful stained glass windows imported from France via the Santa Fe Trail Wikipedia
The legend of the mysterious carpenter, often attributed to St. Joseph Official Site
A historically significant landmark listed on the National Register of Historic Places NPS
attractions.tips: The chapel is open 364 days a year but may close for private weddings, so check the official calendar before your visit. Photography of the staircase is permitted, though climbing it is strictly prohibited to ensure its preservation Official Site.

attractions.name: Nambé Pueblo (Nanbé Owingeh)
attractions.category: Landmark, National Historic Landmark, Recreation Area
attractions.description: The Pueblo of Nambé is a Tewa-speaking community situated in the foothills of the Sangre de Cristo Mountains, known for its strong agricultural traditions and world-renowned micaceous pottery. It encompasses nearly 20,000 acres of high-desert landscape and features the spectacular Nambé Falls and Lake Recreation Area, a popular destination for hiking, fishing, and camping.
attractions.address: 15A Bay Poe (Governor's Office); 544 Poechunu Poe (Nambé Falls), Santa Fe, NM 87506
attractions.hours: Monday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM (Summer); Tuesday: Closed; Wednesday: Closed; Thursday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM; Friday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM; Saturday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM; Sunday: 7:00 AM – 7:00 PM
attractions.days_open: Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday, Monday (Summer season starting May 21)
attractions.admission: Day Rates: $20 per carload; Fishing: Adults $18 (5 fish limit), Seniors $12, Military/Veterans $15, Children (12 & under) $10; Watercraft: $15/day; Kayak Rentals: $20–$35; Camping: Basic $40, Ramada $60–$80
highlights: Nambé Falls: A stunning double-drop natural waterfall with two distinct hiking trails.
Nambé Lake: Offers outstanding fishing for trout and motorless boating opportunities.
National Historic Landmark: Recognized for its cultural significance and history dating back to the 14th century.
Annual Feast Day: Honors St. Francis of Assisi on October 4th with ceremonial dances and processions.
Buffalo Range: Home to a majestic buffalo herd with tours available by advance appointment.
Traditional Arts: Famous for traditional textiles and the recent revival of micaceous pottery techniques.
attractions.tips: Always carry drinking water as summer temperatures are high. Wear water shoes for the lower falls trail as it involves river crossings. Note that the recreation area is cash or card, but most fees are listed as cash only; arrive before 6:00 PM for entry as gates close at 7:00 PM. Respect the Pueblo's privacy and follow all posted signs regarding photography permits.

The gallery is renowned for its diverse collection of historical and contemporary works, set within a landmark compound that includes an expansive sculpture garden and koi pond. attractions.name: Northern New Mexico Wine Trail (New Mexico Wine)
attractions.description: New Mexico Wine represents the oldest wine-making tradition in the United States, with a history dating back to 1629. The Northern New Mexico Wine Trail, centered around the Santa Fe area, features a collection of award-winning wineries and tasting rooms along the scenic Rio Grande River Valley. These high-altitude vineyards produce a diverse range of varietals, from robust reds like Tempranillo to world-class sparkling wines. (New Mexico Wine)

attractions.region: Northern New Mexico / Rio Grande Valley (New Mexico Wine)
attractions.hours: Varies by individual winery (typically 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM daily); Association Office: Monday – Friday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM (New Mexico Wine)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Varies by individual winery) (New Mexico Wine)
attractions.admission: Free (Trail access); Tasting fees typically range from $10 to $25 per person (New Mexico Wine)
highlights: Oldest wine-producing region in the United States, established in 1629 (New Mexico Wine)
Features over 10 wineries and tasting rooms within easy driving distance of Santa Fe (Tourism Santa Fe)

Host to major annual events like the Santa Fe Wine & Chile Fiesta (Santa Fe Wine & Chile Fiesta)
attractions.tips: Explore downtown Santa Fe tasting rooms like Gruet or Hervé for a convenient introduction to local wines, download the official interactive map from nmwine.com to plan your trail route, and always ensure you have a designated driver for vineyard tours. (New Mexico Wine)

attractions.name: Ohkay Owingeh Pueblo (San Juan Pueblo)
attractions.description: Ohkay Owingeh, formerly known as San Juan Pueblo, is the largest of the Tewa-speaking pueblos in New Mexico and was the first Spanish capital of the region. Settled over 700 years ago, it is known as the "Place of the Strong People" and is famous for its historic adobe dwellings and vibrant cultural traditions.

attractions.region: Rio Arriba County (North of Santa Fe)
attractions.hours: Monday-Friday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Tribal Administration); Pueblo visitor access generally daylight hours (approx. 9:00 AM - 4:00 PM)

highlights: Birthplace of Popé, leader of the 1680 Pueblo Revolt
Historic San Juan Bautista Church with unique Gothic-inspired architecture
Oke-Oweenge Crafts Cooperative showcasing traditional redware pottery
Annual San Juan Feast Day (June 24) featuring traditional dances
Owe’neh Bupingeh, a preserved district of four ancient plazas and adobe homes
Ohkay Owingeh Tribal Lakes offering fishing and recreation
attractions.tips: Always call ahead as tribal lands may close for ceremonies without notice. Photography and sketching require a permit and fee; obey all posted signs and respect residents' privacy. Do not speed through the pueblo area.

attractions.name: Palace of the Governors (Palacio de los Gobernadores)
attractions.description: The Palace of the Governors is a historic adobe structure that served as the seat of government for New Mexico for centuries, dating back to 1610. As the oldest public building in continuous use by European settlers in the continental United States, it now functions as a primary anchor for the New Mexico History Museum and is a designated National Historic Landmark (NM History Museum, Wikipedia).

attractions.region: Santa Fe Historic District / Downtown Plaza

attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed Mondays during Winter Hours: November–April; Closed Jan 1, Thanksgiving, and Christmas) (New Mexico History Museum)
attractions.admission: New Mexico Residents: $7; Non-residents: $12; Children 16 and under: Free; NM Seniors (60+): Free on Wednesdays; NM Residents: Free on First Sunday of each month; Free for all: First Friday of each month 5pm–7pm (New Mexico History Museum)

attractions.tips: Access to the Palace is primarily through the Lincoln Street entrance of the adjoining New Mexico History Museum. Be sure to browse the Native American vendors under the long portal for authentic handmade crafts, and plan for several hours to explore the extensive history exhibits (NM History Museum).

This National Historic Landmark is designed in the traditional Spanish-American colonial style and features flagstone paths, lush landscaping, and a performance stage. It is famously surrounded by historic buildings and serves as the primary venue for major annual events like the Santa Fe Indian Market and Spanish Market.

For authentic handmade goods, check the daily lineup of Native American artisans under the portal of the Palace of the Governors. The Plaza is a great spot for people-watching and is the starting point for many walking tours.
attractions.nearby: New Mexico Museum of Art, Cathedral Basilica of St. Francis of Assisi, Loretto Chapel, Georgia O’Keeffe Museum, Palace of the Governors.

attractions.name: Santa Fe River Park (also known as Santa Fe River Parkway)
attractions.description: A lush, tree-shaded linear park following the Santa Fe River through the heart of the city. Historically used by Santa Fe Trail traders for camping and grazing animals, it now offers a peaceful urban escape with scenic walkways and picnic areas (NPS, Tripadvisor).

Mature shade trees and scenic picnic spots (Tripadvisor)
Access to a wider 10-mile trail system (Airial Travel)
Located near the historic downtown Santa Fe Plaza (Tripadvisor)
attractions.tips: Visit in spring or fall for the best temperatures and potential river flow; clear signage is limited in some sections (Airial Travel).

attractions.name: School for Advanced Research (SAR) School for Advanced Research
attractions.description: The School for Advanced Research (SAR) is a preeminent independent institute in Santa Fe dedicated to the study of anthropology, the humanities, and Native American arts. It houses the Indian Arts Research Center, which stewards an extensive collection of over 12,000 works of Indigenous art from the American Southwest. The institution provides residential fellowships for scholars and artists and operates SAR Press, a major academic publisher in the social sciences. School for Advanced Research, Wikipedia

attractions.city: Santa Fe School for Advanced Research

attractions.description: SITE Santa Fe is a dynamic, interdisciplinary contemporary arts institution that places artists at the center and explores extraordinary ideas through innovative exhibitions and programs. As a non-collecting institution, it remains flexible and responsive to the contemporary art world, frequently commissioning new works and hosting world-class biennials.

attractions.name: Ski Santa Fe (Ski Santa Fe Official Site)
attractions.description: Ski Santa Fe is a premier high-altitude ski destination located just 16 miles from the historic Santa Fe Plaza in the Sangre de Cristo Mountains. With a base elevation of 10,350 feet and a summit of 12,075 feet, it offers a diverse range of terrain including 89 trails and extensive glade skiing for all ability levels. (Ski Santa Fe Official Site, Tripadvisor)

attractions.city: Santa Fe (Ski Santa Fe Official Site)

Inspired by the great onsen resorts of Japan, it offers a serene environment with private outdoor hot tubs, professional massage therapy, and an award-winning izakaya restaurant. (Ten Thousand Waves About, Spas of America)

attractions.name: Tesoros de Devoción: Objects of Devotion
attractions.description: Tesoros de Devoción (Treasures of Devotion) is a permanent exhibition that explores the rich tradition of New Mexican devotional art through a vast collection of bultos, retablos, and crucifijos dating from the late 1700s to 1900. It showcases how self-taught local artisans blended European and Mexican colonial iconography with regional materials and styles to create a unique cultural hybrid that remains a source of pride for New Mexican Hispanic communities (New Mexico History Museum).
attractions.address: 113 Lincoln Avenue, Santa Fe, NM 87501
attractions.hours: Sunday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Monday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Thursday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM, Friday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (Summer: 10:00 AM – 7:00 PM, May–October), Saturday: 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM (New Mexico History Museum)
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed January 1, Thanksgiving, and Christmas) (New Mexico History Museum)
attractions.admission: Non-residents: $12.00, New Mexico Residents: $7.00, Children (16 and under): Free, NM Residents (First Sunday of each month): Free, NM Seniors (Wednesdays): Free, Friday Evenings (5:00 PM – 7:00 PM, May–October): Free for all (New Mexico History Museum)
highlights: The Larry and Alyce Frank Collection of Santos featuring over 60 significant works
Hand-carved bultos (sculptures) and painted retablos (wooden panels) dating back to 1750
Rare examples of tanned buckskin paintings depicting saints and religious scenes
The 18th-century Mexican Period capilla (chapel) housed within the Palace of the Governors
Esoteric pieces such as the 'Crucifixion in a Large Nicho' by the Laguna Santero

attractions.tips: Visit between 5:00 PM and 7:00 PM on Friday evenings from May through October for free admission. New Mexico residents can enjoy free entry all day on the first Sunday of every month. Be sure to explore the Portal of the Palace of the Governors outside, where Native American artisans sell handmade work as part of a traditional museum program (New Mexico History Museum).

From the flagship Alegría taproom with its spacious music courtyard to the creative Pottery Pub near the Plaza, the venue offers everything from live entertainment and scratch-made cocktails to hands-on pottery experiences. attractions.name: Valles Caldera National Preserve
attractions.description: Valles Caldera National Preserve protects a spectacular 13.7-mile-wide volcanic crater created by a massive eruption 1.2 million years ago. The preserve is celebrated for its expansive mountain meadows, abundant wildlife including one of New Mexico's largest elk herds, and a rich human history spanning ancestral native homelands to historic ranching operations National Park Service, National Park Foundation.
attractions.address: 39201 New Mexico Highway 4
attractions.region: Jemez Mountains, Santa Fe Area National Park Service
attractions.hours: Summer (Memorial Day to Labor Day): 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM; Winter: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Pedestrian access: Dawn to dusk daily National Park Service
attractions.days_open: Daily (Closed Thanksgiving and Christmas Day) National Park Service
attractions.admission: Private Vehicle: $25.00, Motorcycle: $20.00, Per Person: $15.00, Annual Park Pass: $45.00 (Free with America the Beautiful passes) National Park Service
highlights: Exploring the 14-mile-wide volcanic crater and its resurgent lava domes National Park Service
Wildlife viewing for elk, Gunnison prairie dogs, and golden eagles National Park Service
Visiting the historic Cabin District, a filming location for the TV series Longmire TravelAwaits
Hiking and mountain biking across miles of scenic trails like the Cerro La Jara Loop National Park Service
Fly fishing for wild brown trout in the preserve's meandering streams National Park Service
Stargazing in an International Dark Sky Park with some of the world's darkest skies National Park Service
attractions.tips: Bring your own food and water as services are limited; fill your gas tank in Los Alamos or Jemez Springs before arriving. Be prepared for high-altitude conditions (8,000+ feet) and download maps in advance as cell service is unreliable TravelAwaits. Reserve backcountry vehicle permits in advance via Recreation.gov if planning to drive deep into the preserve National Park Service.

attractions.name: Vivác Winery
attractions.description: Vivác (Vee Vok) is a mountaineering word meaning "high altitude refuge," situated at 6,000 feet as one of the highest altitude wineries in the world. This award-winning winery features hand-crafted wines produced in North America's oldest grape-growing region. The estate includes a 900-square-foot adobe tasting room built by the winemakers themselves, surrounded by scenic vineyards and gardens. Vivac Winery
